channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Navigating Infertility in Canada: A Personal IVF Journey and Systemic Gaps
item.description: Kathryn Blaze Baum recounts her family’s arduous journey with infertility and in vitro fertilization (IVF) in Canada. After conceiving her first daughter naturally, she experienced a miscarriage in 20...

item.title: Government Plans Early Market Closures Amid Energy Crisis, Keeps Restaurants Open Till 10pm
item.description: Federal Minister for Petroleum Ali Pervaiz Malik informed Geo News that the Pakistani government is contemplating early closure of markets as part of austerity measures due to the ongoing global energ...

item.title: Bikaner Namkeen Exports Disrupted Due to US-Iran Conflict
item.description: The ongoing US-Iran conflict is causing serious concerns for traders in Bikaner, especially those involved in exporting popular snacks, spices, and namkeen to Gulf and European countries. Exporters re...

item.title: Trump’s Escalation in Iran War Increases Global Economic Strain
item.description: As President Donald Trump reinforced the U.S. stance in the ongoing conflict with Iran, global markets reacted with caution. The prolonged war has caused disruptions in oil and gas supplies, leading t...

item.title: Grey's Anatomy Episode 16 Recap: Bailey Faces Consequences and Relationships Are Tested
item.description: In the April 2 episode of 'Grey's Anatomy,' Dr. Miranda Bailey finds herself in a serious predicament after covering up an FDA-prohibited experimental treatment administered by Kwan to a patient. Init...

item.title: Tiger Woods arrested for DUI following rollover crash, cites prescription medication
item.description: Golf legend Tiger Woods was arrested for driving under the influence after a rollover accident in Jupiter Island, Florida, on Friday afternoon. His Land Rover SUV collided with the rear of a truck and...

item.title: Major Companies Shift Operations from New York to Sun Belt States Amid Tax and Cost Pressures
item.description: In early 2026, New York City experienced a significant outflow of major corporations and financial firms, with nearly 5,000 businesses relocating or expanding to lower-tax states such as Florida and T...

item.title: 5.9-Magnitude Earthquake in Afghanistan Kills 8; Tremors Felt Across Northern India
item.description: On Friday evening, a 5.9-magnitude earthquake struck Afghanistan's Hindu Kush region, resulting in the deaths of at least eight people and injuring a child in Kabul after a house collapsed. The seismi...

item.title: Reflections on Grief and Emotional Regulation
item.description: The article explores how experiencing grief can provide insight into managing emotions effectively. It emphasizes that grief, though painful, can serve as a learning experience for emotional regulatio...

item.title: Two Dead and One Injured in N1 Collision Outside Polokwane Amid Easter Traffic
item.description: Over the Easter weekend, a tragic collision on the N1 near Polokwane claimed the lives of two individuals and left another person injured. Traffic authorities have warned motorists travelling from Gau...

item.title: Exploring Zanzibar’s Rangi za Mora: Tradition Meets Contemporary Fashion
item.description: On a breezy Zanzibar evening, Rangi za Mora gathered designers, performers and guests for a fashion event that goes beyond runway shows. Now in its third edition, this showcase blends Zanzibar’s Afric...

item.title: Jon Stewart Discusses Concerns Over Trump's Leadership and Decision-Making
item.description: Comedian and political commentator Jon Stewart expressed deep concern over President Donald Trump's apparent indifference to the consequences of his actions. Speaking on his podcast 'The Weekly Show,'...

item.title: Easter Egg Prices Soar Amid Rising Production Costs
item.description: This Easter, the cost of chocolate eggs in the UK has surged due to rising production expenses, with some treats priced as high as £25. The average Easter egg now costs £3.27, marking a 9% increase on...

item.title: Potential Leak of Sensitive CBP Facility Information Through Public Flashcards
item.description: A publicly available set of Quizlet flashcards may have exposed sensitive information related to U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) facilities in Kingsville, Texas. The flashcards, created in Fe...

item.title: Three Individuals Charged After Arson Attack on Jewish Charity Ambulances in North London
item.description: Three people have been formally charged following the deliberate setting alight of four ambulances owned by a Jewish charity in Golders Green, north London, on 23 March. The vehicles, operated by Hatz...

item.title: Chinese Space Pioneer’s Tianlong 3 Rocket Fails on Maiden Launch
item.description: On April 3, 2026, at 04:17 UTC, the first launch of Space Pioneer's Tianlong 3 rocket ended in failure. The launch took place from the company’s site in Dongfeng, Inner Mongolia, near the Jiuquan Spac...

item.title: Pope Leo XIV Advocates Diplomacy and Humanitarian Efforts Amid Conflicts in Israel and Ukraine
item.description: On Good Friday, Pope Leo XIV emphasized the need for renewed diplomatic efforts to resolve conflicts in the Middle East and Ukraine through separate phone conversations with Israeli President Isaac He...

item.title: French container ship granted passage through Strait of Hormuz amid Iran conflict
item.description: A French container ship has become the first major western vessel to navigate safely through the Strait of Hormuz since the outbreak of the Iran war five weeks ago. The ship, operated by CMA CGM and o...
